title: SDA Selects Vendor to Deliver Advanced Fire Control Ground Infrastructure
text:
Date: Nov. 12, 2024
WASHINGTON, D.C. – The Space Development Agency (SDA) today announced a contract for Advanced Fire Control Ground Infrastructure (AFCGI) to support demonstrations and potential future operations under the agency’s Advanced Fire Control (AFC) effort. The cost-plus award fee contract worth a maximum of approximately $117 million was awarded to Kratos Technology & Training Solutions, Inc., San Diego.
As the AFCGI vendor, Kratos will lead a team of industry performers to provide a common, enduring ground infrastructure and resources to minimize cost and complexity for multiple AFC prototype efforts. Delivery of ground infrastructure by the AFCGI vendor will be critical to the successful execution of the Fire-control On Orbit-support to the warfighter (FOO Fighter or F2) prototype system and future fire-control demonstration programs.
Initial funding of approximately $17 million was obligated at the time of award, using both fiscal year 2024 and fiscal year 2025 Research, Development, Test & Evaluation funds. Space Development Agency, Washington, DC, is the contracting activity (FA240125C0004).
About the Space Development Agency. As part of the U.S. Space Force, SDA is recognized as DOD’s constructive disruptor for space acquisition. SDA will accelerate delivery of needed space-based capabilities to the joint warfighter to support terrestrial missions through development, fielding, and operation of the Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture. For more information on SDA, contact OSD.SDA.Outreach@mail.mil or visit https://www.sda.mil.
-END-
